title: agnus-castus vulgaris var. alba
intro: Agnus-castus vulgaris var. alba, also known as white Chasteberry, is a plant species in the Vitex genus, traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have a balancing effect on hormone production, particularly in relation to estrogen and progesterone levels, making it a popular natural remedy for menstrual irregularities and menopausal symptoms. The plant's health benefits also include its potential to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS), as well as its possible anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Agnus-castus vulgaris var. alba is often prepared as a dried herb, capsule, or tincture, and can be taken internally to support hormonal balance and overall well-being.
